parts.answer: • toxic hazard symbol drawn Allow any representation of skull and crossbones Some representation of a face and two crossed lines is the minimum Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark following points: • (from) orange • (to) green (1) (1) Allow to blue Allow 1 mark for colours reversed Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark

A diagram that shows: • round-bottomed/pear shaped flask containing the mixture with heat label • anti-bumping granules in round- bottomed flask • (labelled) sloping condenser with correct direction of water in and out • collection vessel and system sealed on the left and open on the right (1) (1) (1) (1) Example of a diagram: Allow any indication of heat including just an arrow Allow just arrows showing the water direction Ignore inclusion of thermometer/dropping funnel even if incorrectly placed The condenser should have some indication of a water jacket Incorrect labelling, e.g. a round bottom flask labelled as a conical flask, would not be awarded the corresponding mark (M1 in this case) Max 3 for one-piece apparatus NB reflux set up would be able to score M1 and M2 Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • (alcohols/products are) flammable / may ignite / can burn Ignore more even heating/avoids hot-spots Ignore easier to control the temperature Ignore heat more safely Ignore references to explosions Ignore references to fire as a hazard Do not award potassium dichromate is flammable Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark (2-methylpropan-2-ol resists oxidation because 2- methylpropan-2-ol is a) tertiary (alcohol) / 3 Allow tertiary alcohols are resistant to oxidation Allow tertiary (alcohols) cannot be oxidised Ignore no reaction Ignore minor errors in the alcohols name Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • identification of the three oxidation products Additional guidance: Propanal / CH CH CHO and propanoic acid / CH CH COOH (from propan-1-ol) and propanone / CH COCH (from propan-2-ol) Accept skeletal/displayed formulae Allow propan-1-al and propan-2-one Ignore carbon dioxide/CO and water/H O If both name and formula are given, then both must be correct (Total for Question 4 = 13 marks) (Total for Paper = 50 marks) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark the following points: • test for propanal/aldehyde • result for propanal • test for (propanoic) acid • res
